./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev ./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\\n1\\t//!\\n2\\t//! # Layers\\n3\\t//!\\n4\\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\\n5\\t//!\\n6\\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`) control access to\\n7\\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\\n8\\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\\n9\\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\\n10\\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\\n15\\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\\n16\\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\\n17\\t//!\\n18\\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\\n21\\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\\n22\\t//!\\n23\\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\\n24\\t//!\\n25\\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\\\"*\\\"` for all databases).\\n26\\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\\n27\\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\\n28\\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\\n29\\t\\n30\\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\n31\\t\\n32\\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\\n33\\t\\n34\\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\\n35\\t///\\n36\\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`.\\n37\\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\\n38\\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\\n39\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n40\\tpub enum Role {\\n41\\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\\n42\\t    None = 0,\\n43\\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\\n44\\t    Read = 1,\\n45\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\\n46\\t    Write = 2,\\n47\\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\\n48\\t    Admin = 3,\\n49\\t}\\n50\\t\\n51\\timpl Role {\\n52\\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\\n53\\t    ///\\n54\\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\\n55\\t    ///\\n56\\t    /// | Input | Role |\\n57\\t    /// |-------|------|\\n58\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-admin\\\"` / `\\\"tag:admin\\\"` / `\\\"admin\\\"` | `Admin` |\\n59\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-write\\\"` / `\\\"tag:axon-agent\\\"` / `\\\"tag:write\\\"` / `\\\"write\\\"` | `Write` |\\n60\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-read\\\"` / `\\\"tag:read\\\"` / `\\\"read\\\"` | `Read` |\\n61\\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\\n62\\t    ///\\n63\\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\\n64\\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\\n65\\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: \u0026str) -\u003e Self {\\n66\\t        match tag {\\n67\\t            \\\"tag:axon-admin\\\" | \\\"tag:admin\\\" | \\\"admin\\\" =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n68\\t            \\\"tag:axon-write\\\" | \\\"tag:axon-agent\\\" | \\\"tag:write\\\" | \\\"write\\\" =\u003e Role::Write,\\n69\\t            \\\"tag:axon-read\\\" | \\\"tag:read\\\" | \\\"read\\\" =\u003e Role::Read,\\n70\\t            _ =\u003e Role::None,\\n71\\t        }\\n72\\t    }\\n73\\t\\n74\\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\\n75\\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: \u0026[String]) -\u003e Self {\\n76\\t        tags.iter()\\n77\\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\\n78\\t            .max()\\n79\\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\\n80\\t    }\\n81\\t}\\n82\\t\\n83\\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\\n84\\t    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {\\n85\\t        match self {\\n86\\t            Role::None =\u003e write!(f, \\\"none\\\"),\\n87\\t            Role::Read =\u003e write!(f, \\\"read\\\"),\\n88\\t            Role::Write =\u003e write!(f, \\\"write\\\"),\\n89\\t            Role::Admin =\u003e write!(f, \\\"admin\\\"),\\n90\\t        }\\n91\\t    }\\n92\\t}\\n93\\t\\n94\\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\\n95\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\n96\\tpub enum Operation {\\n97\\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\\n98\\t    Read,\\n99\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\\n100\\t    Write,\\n101\\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\\n102\\t    Admin,\\n103\\t}\\n104\\t\\n105\\timpl Operation {\\n106\\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\\n107\\t    pub fn required_role(self) -\u003e Role {\\n108\\t        match self {\\n109\\t            Operation::Read =\u003e Role::Read,\\n110\\t            Operation::Write =\u003e Role::Write,\\n111\\t            Operation::Admin =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n112\\t        }\\n113\\t    }\\n114\\t}\\n115\\t\\n116\\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\\n117\\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\\n118\\t///\\n119\\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\\n120\\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\\n121\\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\\n122\\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\\n123\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n124\\tpub struct EntityFilter {\\n125\\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\\n126\\t    pub field: String,\\n127\\t    /// The required value for that field.\\n128\\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\\n129\\t}\\n130\\t\\n131\\timpl EntityFilter {\\n132\\t    /// Convenience constructor.\\n133\\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, value: serde_json::Value) -\u003e Self {\\n134\\t        Self {\\n135\\t            field: field.into(),\\n136\\t            value,\\n137\\t        }\\n138\\t    }\\n139\\t\\n140\\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\\n141\\t    ///\\n142\\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\\n143\\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\\n144\\t    pub fn matches(\u0026self, data: \u0026serde_json::Value) -\u003e bool {\\n145\\t        data.get(\u0026self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == \u0026self.value)\\n146\\t    }\\n147\\t}\\n148\\t\\n149\\t/// Identity context for a request.\\n150\\t///\\n151\\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\\n152\\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\\n153\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n154\\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\\n155\\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\\n156\\t    pub actor: String,\\n157\\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\\n158\\t    pub role: Role,\\n159\\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\\n160\\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\\n161\\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\\n162\\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \\\"Option::is_none\\\")]\\n163\\t    pub entity_filter: Option\u003cEntityFilter\u003e,\\n164\\t}\\n165\\t\\n166\\timpl CallerIdentity {\\n167\\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\\n168\\t    pub fn anonymous() -\u003e Self {\\n169\\t        Self {\\n170\\t            actor: \\\"anonymous\\\".into(),\\n171\\t            role: Role::Admin,\\n172\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n173\\t        }\\n174\\t    }\\n175\\t\\n176\\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\\n177\\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, role: Role) -\u003e Self {\\n178\\t        Self {\\n179\\t            actor: actor.into(),\\n180\\t            role,\\n181\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n182\\t        }\\n183\\t    }\\n184\\t\\n185\\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\\n186\\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -\u003e Self {\\n187\\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\\n188\\t        self\\n189\\t    }\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\\n192\\t    ///\\n193\\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\\n194\\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\\n195\\t    pub fn check(\u0026self, op: Operation) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n196\\t        let required = op.required_role();\\n197\\t        if self.role \u003e= required {\\n198\\t            Ok(())\\n199\\t        } else {\\n200\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n201\\t                \\\"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\\\",\\n202\\t                self.role, op, required\\n203\\t            )))\\n204\\t        }\\n205\\t    }\\n206\\t\\n207\\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\\n208\\t    ///\\n209\\t    /// Admin users see all fields. axon_audit::entry::AuditEntry;\\nuse axon_core::error::AxonError;\\nuse axon_core::id::{\\n    CollectionId, EntityId, Namespace, QualifiedCollectionId, DEFAULT_DATABASE, DEFAULT_SCHEMA,\\n};\\nuse axon_core::types::Entity;\\nuse axon_schema::schema::{CollectionSchema, CollectionView};\\n\\nuse crate::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n/// PostgreSQL-backed storage adapter.\\n///\\n/// Uses the asynchronous `tokio-postgres` crate driven from a dedicated Tokio\\n/// runtime. Because `StorageAdapter` is synchronous, each database call\\n/// blocks the calling thread via `handle.block_on(...)`.\\n///\\n/// Transactions are handled via `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` / `ROLLBACK` statements.\\n/// The adapter creates the required tables on initialization if they do not\\n/// exist.\\npub struct PostgresStorageAdapter {\\n    client: tokio_postgres::Client,\\n    handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,\\n    _rt: tokio::runtime::Runtime,\\n    in_tx: bool,\\n}\\n\\nimpl PostgresStorageAdapter {\\n    /// Connect to a PostgreSQL database using a connection string.\\n    ///\\n    /// Example: `\\\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon\\\"`\\n    pub fn connect(params: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cSelf, AxonError\u003e {\\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\\n            .worker_threads(2)\\n            .enable_all()\\n            .build()\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let handle = rt.handle().clone();\\n        let (client, connection) = handle\\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(params, NoTls))\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(format!(\\\"connection failed: {e}\\\")))?;\\n        handle.spawn(async move {\\n            if let Err(e) = connection.await {\\n                tracing::error!(\\\"postgres connection error: {e}\\\");\\n            }\\n        });\\n        let mut adapter = Self {\\n            client,\\n            handle,\\n            _rt: rt,\\n            in_tx: false,\\n        };\\n        adapter.init_schema()?;\\n        Ok(adapter)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Block the current thread on a `tokio_postgres` future, converting any\\n    /// error into `AxonError::Storage`.\\n    ///\\n    /// When called from within a Tokio async task, uses\\n    /// [`tokio::task::block_in_place`] to temporarily un-enter the async\\n    /// context before blocking on the adapter's dedicated runtime. We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\\\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\\\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to connect() -\u003e Result\u003cSelf, TestSetupError\u003e {\\n            if let Ok(url) = std::env::var(\\\"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES\\\") {\\n                return Ok(Self {\\n                    url,\\n                    _container: None,\\n                });\\n            }\\n\\n            let container = PgContainer::default()\\n                .with_db_name(\\\"axon_test\\\")\\n                .with_user(\\\"postgres\\\")\\n                .with_password(\\\"postgres\\\")\\n                .start()\\n                .map_err(|error| {\\n                    TestSetupError::Skip(format!(\\n postgres_test_guard() -\u003e MutexGuard\u003c'static, ()\u003e {\\n        static GUARD: OnceLock\u003cMutex\u003c()\u003e\u003e = OnceLock::new();\\n        GUARD\\n            .get_or_init(|| Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n//!\\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n//!\\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n//!\\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n//!\\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n//!\\n//! # Request flow\\n//!\\n//! ```text\\n//! TCP accept\\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n//!             └─ route handler\\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n//!                  └─ handler logic\\n//! ```\\n//!\\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\npub mod actor_scope;\\npub mod auth;\\nmod collection_listing;\\npub(crate) mod axum::extract::Extension;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde::Serialize;\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ISSUER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"test-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"user-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"super-secret-key-for-tests\\\";\\nconst TEST_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_claims() -\u003e JwtClaims {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nfn make_storage() -\u003e MemoryStorageAdapter {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Test User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    storage\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let storage: Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n        Box::new(make_storage());\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service call should succeed\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .expect(\\\"body read should succeed\\\");\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn get_req(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer_req(path: \u0026str, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn error_code(json: \u0026Value) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n    json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\")\\n}\\n\\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[test]\\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\\n}\\n\\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"unauthenticated\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\\n assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"credential_malformed\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\\n     databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    };\\n    let token = Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\n//!\\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\\n    let state = URL says \\\"acme\\\"\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\\n    };\\n    let token = .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"credential_wrong_tenant\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.output:
---
name: frontend-design
description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt
---

This skill guides creation of distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic "AI slop" aesthetics. Implement real working code with exceptional attention to aesthetic details and creative choices.

The user provides frontend requirements: a component, page, application, or interface to build. They may include context about the purpose, audience, or technical constraints.

## Design Thinking

Before coding, understand the context and commit to a BOLD aesthetic direction:
- **Purpose**: What problem does this interface solve? Who uses it?
- **Tone**: Pick an extreme: brutally minimal, maximalist chaos, retro-futuristic, organic/natural, luxury/refined, playful/toy-like, editorial/magazine, brutalist/raw, art deco/geometric, soft/pastel, industrial/utilitarian, etc. There are so many flavors to choose from. Use these for inspiration but design one that is true to the aesthetic direction.
- **Constraints**: Technical requirements (framework, performance, accessibility).
- **Differentiation**: What makes this UNFORGETTABLE? What's the one thing someone will remember?

**CRITICAL**: Choose a clear conceptual direction and execute it with precision. Bold maximalism and refined minimalism both work - the key is intentionality, not intensity.

Then implement working code (HTML/CSS/JS, React, Vue, etc.) that is:
- Production-grade and functional
- Visually striking and memorable
- Cohesive with a clear aesthetic point-of-view
- Meticulously refined in every detail

## Frontend Aesthetics Guidelines

Focus on:
- **Typography**: Choose fonts that are beautiful, unique, and interesting. Avoid generic fonts like Arial and Inter; opt instead for distinctive choices that elevate the frontend's aesthetics; unexpected, characterful font choices. Pair a distinctive display font with a refined body font.
- **Color & Theme**: Commit to a cohesive aesthetic. Use CSS variables for consistency. Dominant colors with sharp accents outperform timid, evenly-distributed palettes.
- **Motion**: Use animations for effects and micro-interactions. Prioritize CSS-only solutions for HTML. Use Motion library for React when available. Focus on high-impact moments: one well-orchestrated page load with staggered reveals (animation-delay) creates more delight than scattered micro-interactions. Use scroll-triggering and hover states that surprise.
- **Spatial Composition**: Unexpected layouts. Asymmetry. Overlap. Diagonal flow. Grid-breaking elements. Generous negative space OR controlled density.
- **Backgrounds & Visual Details**: Create atmosphere and depth rather than defaulting to solid colors. Add contextual effects and textures that match the overall aesthetic. Apply creative forms like gradient meshes, noise textures, geometric patterns, layered transparencies, dramatic shadows, decorative borders, custom cursors, and grain overlays.

NEVER use generic AI-generated aesthetics like overused font families (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system fonts), cliched color schemes (particularly purple gradients on white backgrounds), predictable layouts and component patterns, and cookie-cutter design that lacks context-specific character.

Interpret creatively and make unexpected choices that feel genuinely designed for the context. No design should be the same. Vary between light and dark themes, different fonts, different aesthetics. NEVER converge on common choices (Space Grotesk, for example) across generations.

**IMPORTANT**: Match implementation complexity to the aesthetic vision. Maximalist designs need elaborate code with extensive animations and effects. Minimalist or refined designs need restraint, precision, and careful attention to spacing, typography, and subtle details. Elegance comes from executing the vision well.

Remember: Claude is capable of extraordinary creative work. Don't hold back, show what can truly be created when thinking outside the box and committing fully to a distinctive vision.
